<!-- Generated by the 2026-07-19 multi-agent consolidation audit (13 agents, read-only).
     Every medium+ finding was independently adversarially verified against the actual files
     and (where applicable) the live database. Verdicts shown per finding. -->

# 04 — Security Audit (both repos, verified)

Scope: secrets in git + full history, both Supabase postures (RLS, write paths,
functions), XSS sinks, token handling, rate limiting, email worker auth, headers/CSP.
Severity honestly calibrated. `[CONFIRMED]` / `[REFUTED]` = independent verify-agent
verdict from re-reading the actual files / live DB.

## Verdict summary

| ID | Sev | Repo | Finding | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SEC-01 | Medium | RP | No CSP + CDN scripts without SRI on PII/admin pages | **CONFIRMED** (verify also caught three.js r128 injected without SRI at index.html:1749) |
| SEC-02 | Medium | DD | Direct anon table writes, no rate limiting (violates ADR-002) | **CONFIRMED** (every evidence line re-verified) |
| SEC-03 | Medium | RP | Possible leftover anon INSERT WITH CHECK(true) policies | **REFUTED** — live `pg_policies` on `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof` shows exactly ONE policy in public schema (anon SELECT on `launch_zones`); zero anon write policies anywhere. The contradicting delta-report line is a stale pre-hardening advisor snapshot. Doc fix only. |
| SEC-04 | Low | RP | Unescaped `zip_code` via innerHTML on /account (self-XSS) | reported (fix: add `esc()` like admin/partners) |
| SEC-05 | Low | RP | CSV formula/DDE injection in admin exports | reported (one-line `cell()` fix) |
| SEC-06 | Low | RP | Admin token rotation pending after 2026-07-07 chat exposure | reported (founder-gated; SQL documented in BACKEND.md) |
| SEC-07 | Low | DD | `.env` committed, `.gitignore` doesn't cover it | reported (moot on archive) |
| SEC-08 | Low | RP | `?token=` in URLs, no history scrub | reported (mitigated by Referrer-Policy + noindex + robots; add `history.replaceState`) |
| SEC-09 | Info | RP | POSITIVE: no server-side secrets in repo or full git history | grep across all refs clean |
| SEC-10 | Info | RP | POSITIVE: RPC-only writes, DB rate limiting, escaped admin rendering, authed email worker, security headers | preserve these patterns |
| SEC-11 | Info | DD | POSITIVE: auth middleware / has_role / SECURITY DEFINER hygiene correctly built | bank as Gate-C RBAC reference |

## Full findings

### SEC-01 · Medium · RP — No Content-Security-Policy; CDN scripts without SRI **[CONFIRMED]**
Both public sites pull GSAP + Lenis from cdnjs/jsdelivr via plain `<script src>` with no
`integrity` attribute, and neither `netlify.toml` sets a Content-Security-Policy header
(verify pass adds: three.js r128 is also dynamically injected from cdnjs at
`index.html:1749` without SRI). A CDN compromise could inject arbitrary JS into pages
that collect lead PII and, on `/admin`, hold a live admin token in sessionStorage and
render full lead/partner exports. Other headers (X-Frame-Options DENY, nosniff,
Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) are present and good; CSP and
Strict-Transport-Security are the gaps.
**Evidence:** `index.html:1196-1198`, `gorocketplate/index.html:819-821`,
`netlify.toml:12-18`, `gorocketplate/netlify.toml:3-9`.
**Fix:** SRI hashes on all four CDN tags (or self-host under `/assets/` with the
immutable cache), plus CSP + HSTS headers in both netlify.toml files.

### SEC-02 · Medium · DD — Direct anon people-data writes, no rate limiting **[CONFIRMED]**
DD inserts/updates `leads`, `waitlist_signups`, `restaurant_partner_applications`
directly from the browser with the anon key; migrations grant table-level INSERT to anon.
No `submission_log`, no `rate_limit_guard` — unlimited scripted spam is possible, anon
can set system-managed columns, and the duplicate path issues an anon UPDATE with **no
UPDATE policy** — silently updating 0 rows while the UI reports "We updated your
preferences." Exactly the anti-pattern ADR-002 retired.
**Evidence:** `src/lib/leads.ts:155` (insert), `:161-183` (dead update),
`src/lib/waitlist.ts:34`, migrations `20260707213135` lines 72-83, `20260707222144`
lines 137-144.
**Fix:** decommission the DD backend per the migration plan (06). If it must live on,
port RP's RPC + rate-limit hardening first.

### SEC-03 · Medium · RP — Doc/advisor discrepancy on anon INSERT policies **[REFUTED — RP is clean]**
BACKEND.md claims zero anon write policies; the delta report's advisor line seemed to
contradict it. Live verification settled it: `pg_policies` on `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`
contains exactly one public-schema policy (anon/authenticated **SELECT** on
`launch_zones`). Zero anon INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies exist on any people table.
The delta line is a stale pre-hardening snapshot. **Action: one-line doc fix in the
delta report; no security change needed.**

### SEC-04 · Low · RP — Unescaped `zip_code` innerHTML render on /account (self-XSS)
`account/index.html:107-110` interpolates `l.zip_code` into innerHTML with no `esc()`
helper (admin and partners both have one). Server validates only length 3–12, not
numeric. Impact limited to self-XSS (victim == token holder), but it's an inconsistency
that invites a worse regression if the markup is reused.
**Fix:** add the same `esc()` used elsewhere; enforce numeric ZIP server-side.

### SEC-05 · Low · RP — CSV formula/DDE injection in admin exports
`admin/index.html:164-169` `toCSV()` quotes commas/quotes/newlines but doesn't
neutralize leading `=`, `+`, `-`, `@`, tab. Publicly-submitted fields (business_name,
names, notes) flow into the CSV the operator opens in Excel/Sheets.
**Fix:** one-line guard in `cell()` — prefix dangerous leading chars with `'`.

### SEC-06 · Low · RP — Admin token rotation pending
The current admin token was pasted into a chat transcript 2026-07-07; rotation SQL is
documented (BACKEND.md:84-85) but hasn't been run. Founder-gated.

### SEC-07 · Low · DD — Committed `.env`
Anon/publishable key only (public by design), but `.gitignore` doesn't cover `.env`, so
any future secret added there would auto-commit. Moot once DD is archived; noted for
hygiene.

### SEC-08 · Low · RP — Access tokens in URL query strings
`/account?token=` and `/partners/?token=` magic links persist in browser history and
could be shoulder-surfed; no `history.replaceState` scrub (grep: 0 hits). Meaningfully
mitigated: `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin` (no cross-origin leak),
noindex + robots disallow. Acceptable for pilot; add a scrub + move to Supabase Auth
magic links at Gate C.

### SEC-09 · Info · RP — POSITIVE: clean secret posture
Full-history grep across all refs: only the publishable key appears (annotated as
public in code); no service_role / sb_secret / Resend / Stripe keys ever committed; no
`.env` in RP. Preserve.

### SEC-10 · Info · RP — POSITIVE: hardened production patterns
All public writes via validated SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (every backend call in every page
is `/rest/v1/rpc/*` — zero direct table endpoints); DB-level IP rate limiting with
friendly client cooldown; consistent `esc()` on admin/partners renders; email worker
requires service-role Bearer (unauth POST → 401, selftest exposes booleans only);
private counts only via the intentional `zip_wait_count` aggregate; security headers on
both sites. This is the posture to preserve through consolidation.

### SEC-11 · Info · DD — POSITIVE: DD's auth primitives are well built
`auth-middleware.ts` JWT validation, server-isolated service-role client with
Proxy-lazy guard, `has_role()` SECURITY DEFINER with pinned search_path and revoked
EXECUTE, trigger helpers revoked from PUBLIC, `security_invoker` view. The gap was the
anon table grants (SEC-02), not the auth code. **Bank `user_roles` + `has_role` as the
reference design for the Gate-C RBAC migration ADR-002 already schedules.**

## Consolidated security posture after the migration plan (06) executes
- Single backend (`uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`), RPC-only, rate-limited, advisor-clean.
- DD backend decommissioned (kills SEC-02/07 by construction) after the data-export gate.
- CSP + SRI + HSTS layered on (SEC-01), `esc()` + CSV guard (SEC-04/05), token scrub (SEC-08).
- Founder actions: admin token rotation (SEC-06), formal legal/TCPA before SMS sends.
